summary: Replaced raw major-scene numeric IDs throughout game-mode code and scene data tables with existing MJ_* enum names, including sentinel use of MJ_COUNT. The PR also clarified a couple of function parameter names related to pending/current major scenes. The decomp bot reported no GALE01 binary changes, so this was a semantics-preserving readability/header cleanup rather than a behavior change.
what_changed: Converted major-scene comparisons and switch cases from raw constants such as 0x10, 3, 4, 5, 0x2B, and 0x28 to MJ_* enum values such as MJ_SUPER_SUDDEN_DEATH_VS, MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, MJ_ALLSTAR, MJ_EVENT, and MJ_BOOT.

Updated gm_803DA920 MinorSceneHandler entries in gmscdata.c so the scene IDs use MJ_* enum names instead of hex literals, including entries like MJ_TITLE, MJ_MENU, MJ_VS, MJ_TARGET_TEST, MJ_PROGRESSIVE_SCAN, and MJ_COUNT.
Updated gm_803DACA4 MajorScene entries in gmscdata.c so the major-scene index field uses MJ_* enum values instead of numeric IDs.
Replaced sentinel constants 45 and 0x2D with MJ_COUNT in scene iteration and pending-scene logic.
Changed gm_801A42F8's parameter name from an unnamed/int arg0 style to int pending_major in both gm_1A3F.c and gm_1A3F.h.
Changed gm_801A4340's parameter name from arg0 to major_scene and rewrote its raw-case switch to use named MJ_* values.

Updated scene-transition calls to gm_801A42F8 across gm_1B03.c, gmvsmelee.c, and un_2FC9.c to pass named MJ_* values such as MJ_TITLE, MJ_MENU, MJ_HANYU_CSS, MJ_HANYU_SSS, MJ_TOY_GALLERY, MJ_OPENING_MV, MJ_DEBUG_CUTSCENE, MJ_BOOT, MJ_PROGRESSIVE_SCAN, and MJ_FIXED_CAMERA_VS.
smart_moves: Applied the enum consistently across both executable logic and static scene tables, reducing the chance that future contributors must manually map numeric IDs back to major scenes.
Used MJ_COUNT for loop termination and invalid/sentinel scene values, making table-walk boundaries self-documenting.
Kept the patch value-preserving: the decomp-dev bot reported 'No changes' for GALE01, confirming that symbolic enum substitution did not alter generated code.
Improved parameter names only where the data meaning was clear from usage, e.g. pending_major and major_scene, without over-renaming unrelated internals.
decomp_lessons: When an enum already exists for game-state IDs, prefer the enum name over hex or decimal literals in both control flow and data initializers.
Sentinel values deserve names too: replacing 45 or 0x2D with MJ_COUNT makes scene-table loops and termination conditions easier to audit.
Symbolic constant cleanup can be safely done as a matching-preserving refactor if the enum values match the original literals; verify with the decomp bot.
Scene-transition helper calls such as gm_801A42F8 are easier to understand when the call site says the destination scene directly, e.g. gm_801A42F8(MJ_BOOT) instead of gm_801A42F8(0x28).
Parameter-name improvements in headers should mirror the implementation when a function's role is clear, because headers are the reusable contract for later decomp work.
naming_conventions: Major scene enum values use the MJ_* prefix.
Major-scene enum names are uppercase with underscores and descriptive suffixes, e.g. MJ_SUPER_SUDDEN_DEATH_VS, MJ_CHALLENGER_APPROACH, MJ_PROGRESSIVE_SCAN, MJ_SINGLE_BUTTON_VS.
Game-over major scenes use the _GOVER suffix in names shown by the diff, e.g. MJ_CLASSIC_GOVER, MJ_ADVENTURE_GOVER, MJ_DEBUG_GOVER.
The enum sentinel/count value is named MJ_COUNT.
Function parameters should describe scene meaning when known, e.g. pending_major and major_scene rather than arg0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a constant-name substitution tactic: replace literals with enum constants whose numeric values are identical, preserving generated code.
The patch had balanced line churn in the provided context, 182 added and 182 deleted lines, consistent with a textual refactor.
The decomp-dev bot comment reported 'No changes' for GALE01, providing evidence that the enum refactor matched the prior binary.
No new assembly tricks or control-flow restructures are evidenced; the matching value came from preserving expressions while improving symbolic names.

review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 stated 'No changes', indicating the patch was binary-preserving.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining gm_801A4310 comparisons against raw numeric literals and convert them to MJ_* enum values.
Search for gm_801A42F8 calls that still pass raw constants instead of named major-scene enums.
Audit scene-data tables for remaining numeric major-scene IDs or sentinel values that should use MJ_COUNT.
Check whether minor-scene enums or other game-mode ID enums can receive the same magic-number cleanup.
